mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity earnings revisions higher and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation readings and elevated policy rates compress real disposable income and increase borrowing costs, which suppresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 235 and FEDFUNDS remains above 0.10
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy aggregate supply, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Monthly Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.05 across three consecutive calendar years.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the total addressable market for S&P 500 constituents, directly boosting earnings per share estimates and producing positive forward equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth do not statistically outperform quarters with negative or flat GDP growth (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, which drags down related corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during a 2-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand without triggering inflationary wage spirals, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes, or the combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In an early tightening cycle, rising policy rates increase the cost of capital and compress equity valuation multiples, which systematically produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of periods where the funds rate crosses the 0.10 percent threshold.
